user_query:
I'm evaluating how well game engines translate to real industrial wins in safety training and maintenance guidance. Using the two files in this workspace:
- input/projects.csv (case studies dataset)
- input/engines.json (engine capability notes)

Please produce three outputs under the paths below and make each dependent on the others so I can cross-check the results:

1) outputs/filtered_cases.csv
   - Filter rules (all must match):
     • domain is either "manufacturing" or "industrial"
     • status is either "deployed" or "pilot"
     • year >= 2020
     • title or description contains the word "safety" or "maintenance" (case-insensitive)
   - For each included project, compute:
     • cost_per_participant = cost_usd / participants
     • roi_score = 0.5 * metric_time_saved_percent + 0.5 * metric_error_reduction_percent - (cost_per_participant / 500)
   - Keep all original columns and append the two computed columns. Sort rows by roi_score descending.

2) outputs/stats.json
   - Compute summary metrics across the filtered set only:
     • total_projects (count in input)
     • eligible_projects (count after filtering)
     • counts_by_engine (object of engine -> count)
     • counts_by_modality (object of modality -> count)
     • avg_time_saved_percent (mean of metric_time_saved_percent)
     • avg_error_reduction_percent (mean of metric_error_reduction_percent)
     • median_deployment_time_days
     • total_participants (sum)
     • top_engine_by_eligible_count (the engine name with the highest eligible count)
     • top3_ids_by_roi (list of the top three project ids by roi_score)

3) outputs/report.md
   - Compose a concise engineering status briefing (roughly 300–500 words) that:
     • Summarizes the goal and method in 2–3 sentences.
     • States exactly this line using values from outputs/stats.json: "Summary: X projects considered, Y meet criteria, median deployment time Z days."
     • Highlights the top 3 projects by roi_score as a bulleted list with: (id, title, engine, modality, roi_score rounded to one decimal).
     • Includes a short paragraph comparing the engines represented in the top 3, citing at least one verbatim "strengths" phrase from input/engines.json for each engine mentioned.
     • Concludes with one actionable next step.

Assumptions: No external data or web access is needed. Use only the provided inputs. Ensure the three outputs are internally consistent (e.g., the top3_ids_by_roi in stats.json match the first three rows in outputs/filtered_cases.csv and the bullet list in outputs/report.md). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
